Strategy Termination Process
We are now verifying your strategy termination request. Once the strategy termination process is successfully initiated, the redemptions for all users will be processed at the next redemption window.


Strategy Termination Verification
Your strategy termination application will be processed only if all the following conditions are met:
- No unsettled derivative positions
- No non-USDT spot holdings
- No existing orders
- No unsettled funds

Failure to meet these conditions may result in an unsuccessful fund closure.

copyFutureStopLossDesc:
Perp Copy Stop Loss (PCSL) is a tool to limit risk in your copied USDT perpetual positions when your cumulative losses with a Master Trader in USDT perpetuals reach a specific threshold. You can use PCSL to cap your losses and trigger an automatic closure if their value declines.

For instance, if you choose to follow a Master Trader and set a PCSL at 400 USDT, when your losses with the Master Trader reach 400 USDT, you will automatically unfollow them. Subsequently, your remaining positions will be handled based on your PCSL preferences:

1. Close all copied positions with the Master Trader at the current market price, or
2. Continue to follow the Master Trader and exit when they close their positions.

CopyStopLossExplain:
Copy Stop Loss (CSL) is an instruction to close a copy position if the value of the entire copy position drops below a specific ratio. You can use this to protect your position or to automatically sell the copy position if its value goes down.

For example, you choose to copy a trader with 1,000 USDT and set the CSL at 40%. If the copy position value, including the profit/loss overall trades, drops below 40% of 1,000 USDT, the whole copy position would close, and the remaining margin (600 USDT) would be returned to your balance.

cslIntroduction:
Copy Stop Loss (CSL) is an instruction to limit the risk of your copy positions if your accumulated losses with a Master Trader reach a specific amount. You can use the CSL to protect your copy positions or to automatically sell them if their value goes down.
For example, you choose to copy a Master Trader and set a CSL at 400 USDT. If the accumulated losses of your copy positions with the Master Trader reach 400 USDT, you will automatically unfollow the Master Trader. After which, your remaining positions will be managed according to your preferred CSL settings: 1) Close all copy positions with the Master Trader at the current market price; or 2) Follow the Master Trader and exit when he closes his position.

faqAns1: Copy Trading Pro is a financial product on Bybit that allows users (investors) to invest in strategies created by experienced traders, known as Pro Master. This product is closed-end, opening periodically for investments. Investors can invest by buying shares of Pro Masters' strategies and potentially gain profits without actively managing their trades.
faqAns2: No, Copy Trading Pro is not a principal-protected or guaranteed financial product. Investors may face losses if the Pro Master's strategies underperform.
faqAns3: It is the minimum amount of USDT that Pro Master must deposit to create a strategy. The amount will be locked in the strategy and cannot be withdrawn until the strategy ends or is terminated.
faqAns4: From the 5th to the 25th week of the Lock-up Period, the Pro Master Trader can choose to manually terminate the strategy before its natural expiry.
faqAns5:
Seed Round: Investors provide the initial capital for the strategy. During this phase, the Transfer function of the Copy Trading Pro Subaccount is disabled.
Lock-Up Period: During this period, the Pro Master begins trading with the pooled assets. Assets are locked up and can only be withdrawn during designated window periods.
faqAns6: Net Asset Value (NAV) is the total value (equity in USDT) of a Copy Trading Pro strategy's portfolio, divided by the number of outstanding shares. It is calculated daily from 12AM (midnight) to 11:59PM UTC and updated at 2AM UTC the following day.
faqAns7: You can redeem your shares only during the Redemption Window. The amount you can redeem is based on the NAV calculated at 2AM UTC on the T+6 day. Additionally, shares must be held for at least four (4) calendar days before you can redeem them.

slipSettingDesc:
A copy order will not be initiated if the entry price deviates beyond either the default maximum slippage or the slippage threshold set by the Follower, which is typically represented as a percentage difference from the Master Trader's order filled price. The default slippage varies from 0.5% to 1.5% for different trading pairs.

Note: Setting a lower slippage can protect your copy trade's entry price, but it may increase the risk of copy order failure. As the Follower's order is executed as market orders, the filled price may drift beyond the maximum slippage limits due to market volatility.

trailingStopDesc: Trailing Stop automatically moves up your exit point based on your account's highest recorded equity, rising only with new highs and never moving downward. For example, if your equity fluctuates between 500, 450, 600, 900, 800, 1,000 and 800 at different points throughout the week, and you set a 20% retracement rate, your exit equity will be 400, 400, 480, 720, 720, 800 and 800. When your account equity drops to 800, matching the exit equity, the Trailing Stop will trigger, closing all positions at the market price, and your Copy Trading will be terminated. Please note that your final account equity may differ from the exit equity due to slippage, and data updates may be delayed by up to 10 seconds.
trailingStopExplanationTip:
Trailing Stop automatically moves up your exit point in line with your account’s highest recorded equity, rising only with new highs and never moving downward.

For example, if your equity fluctuates between 500, 450, 600, 900, 800, 1,000 and 800 at different points throughout the week, and you set a 20% retracement rate, your exit equity will be 400, 400, 480, 720, 720, 800 and 800.

When your account equity drops to 800, matching the exit equity, the Trailing Stop will trigger, closing all positions at the market price, and your Copy Trading will be terminated. Please note that your final account equity may differ from the exit equity due to slippage.

usdxTip:
Welcome to TradFi

Your balance is now shown in USDx, an internal display unit on Bybit TradFi that reflects the representative value of your USDT holdings at a 1:1 ratio.

USDx is not legal tender. It is neither a fiat currency (e.g. the US dollar) nor a cryptocurrency. It's non-transferable, non-redeemable and used solely for display and accounting purposes.

Many TradFi platforms show balances and P&L in USD-style formats. USDx follows this convention, making it easier to track and manage your funds at a glance. Your underlying balance remains fully backed by USDT — with no fees, no conversions and no impact on custody.

By using TradFi, you agree to have your balance displayed in USDx for consistent presentation and accurate accounting.
